items.title: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
items.summary: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

items.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
items.summary: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

items.title: Kanye West concert in Poland cancelled over antisemitic comments
items.summary: The UK government blocked the rapper from entering the country over his history of making pro-Nazi and racist statements.

items.title: Thousands celebrate open-air Mass with Pope Leo in Cameroon - in pictures
items.summary: Pope Leo XIV is on his third day in Cameroon before he heads to Angola on Saturday.

items.title: Suspected crime boss Daniel Kinahan arrested in Dubai
items.summary: Kinahan, in his 40s, was arrested in Dubai on foot of an arrest warrant issued by the Irish courts.

items.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
items.summary: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

items.title: Japan reveals new name for 40C-and-hotter days after blistering summer
items.summary: The term - kokushobi - translates to "cruelly hot", "brutally hot" or "severely hot", and comes after Japan's hottest summer on record.

items.title: 'Mum is finally free!' Pensioner detained by ICE in US returns to France
items.summary: Marie-Thérèse had moved to the US after reuniting with her long-lost love, an American man who died in January.

items.title: Palestinians hand over suspect in 1982 attack on Jewish restaurant in Paris
items.summary: Hicham Harb is suspected of directing the attack in the Rue des Rosiers and acting as one of the gunmen who shot at diners.

items.title: Singer D4vd arrested on suspicion of murdering teenage girl
items.summary: The remains of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez were found in the singer's car last year.

items.title: Runaway wolf finally captured after nine-day search in South Korea
items.summary: The search has been marked by twists and turns, gripping South Korea and even inspiring a meme coin.

items.title: Iranian footballers say Australia has given them 'hope' for safe future
items.summary: The duo had sought aslyum after their football team did not sing the national anthem during a match.

items.title: Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model
items.summary: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.

items.title: Naples bank robbers hold 25 people hostage then vanish through tunnel
items.summary: The armed men reportedly evaded capture by escaping through the city's sewer system.

items.title: Three charged with arson on Persian media offices in London
items.summary: Two teenagers and a 21-year-old man appear in court charged with arson with intent to endanger life.

items.title: Pope criticises 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars after Trump spat
items.summary: The comments follow a high-profile spat with US President Donald Trump, who called the Pope weak on crime.

items.title: Rescuers to use air cushions in latest effort to save stranded whale
items.summary: "Timmy" has been stranded in the Baltic Sea for weeks despite several attempts to free the ailing animal.

items.title: Turkish police detain 162 people over online posts about school shootings
items.summary: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.

items.title: Orbán's era was over in a flash and Hungary's next PM is a man in a hurry
items.summary: Péter Magyar and his Tisza party are wasting no time preparing for the transfer of power after their dramatic landslide victory.

items.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
items.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

items.title: What next for South African opposition firebrand Malema after his five-year prison sentence?
items.summary: The sentence raises huge questions marks over the political future of one of South Africa's most controversial politicians.

items.title: 'I was tortured and lost my hand' - one student's struggle to get an education in Nigeria
items.summary: The BBC speaks to a student who pushed for his toe print to be taken to verify his identity.

items.title: Avengers reassemble and Top Gun flies back - Hollywood studios preview their new movies
items.summary: Some of the most hotly anticipated new films of the next couple of years are previewed at CinemaCon.

items.title: School shootings a new trauma for Turkey as nation mourns
items.summary: An expert tells the BBC the attack in Kahramanmaras was a tragedy but "not a surprise".

items.title: Watch: Fuel truck leaves trail of fire on road in Syria
items.summary: Eyewitnesses captured the moment a burning fuel tanker drove through the centre of Hasakah, Syria, leaving a trail of fire behind it.

items.title: BBC in Iran: 'Tehran does not think it has lost this war'
items.summary: Lyse Doucet says Iranians want a solution to the long-running animosity with the US, but leaders is not willing to make a deal on Washington's terms.

items.title: Officer on horseback chases suspect through New York streets
items.summary: The suspect was accused of purse-snatching and has been charged with larceny and providing false information.

items.title: Moment wolf on the run in South Korea is found

items.title: Artemis commander tells BBC about 'powerful' moment crater named after his late wife

items.title: Watch: Rising prices threaten Minnesota's meat raffles

items.title: Artemis II crew describes Moon mission and splashdown moment
